growth_records.medium: Amended high salt minimal (HSM) medium + 0.2% sucrose

up.method: sole added carbon source supporting heterotrophic growth; CFU growth measured

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: 15N net biomass assimilation (N_net) during log-phase growth on sucrose
mfa.units: fraction of biomass newly synthesized (nanoSIMS 15NH4, dimensionless)
mfa.note: Measured N_net matched the expected N_net (~0.99 after 8-13 doublings) during logarithmic growth on sucrose, confirming that each CFU doubling corresponded to a biomass doubling; this is a SIP-derived fraction, NOT a mmol/gDW/h flux

growth_records.cite: Dupuis, Sunnyjoy et al. Scarcity of fixed carbon transfer in a model microbial phototroph-heterotroph interaction. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae140. [PMC11316394]
growth_records.method: reported (CFU/ml over time by plate counts on TY agar; logarithmic-phase growth observed on sucrose; no numeric mu given in text)
growth_records.snippet: the measured values matched the expected values during logarithmic-phase growth on sucrose ... 0.99 N_net for cells grown on sucrose after 8-13 doublings
growth_records.notes: Positive-control heterotrophic growth of the prokaryote M. japonicum on 0.2% sucrose in amended HSM, 28 C, aerobic, batch. No mmol/gDW/h uptake or secretion rate reported; only CFU growth curves and nanoSIMS 15N net-assimilation fractions. N_net=0.99 is a nanoSIMS-derived fraction (biomass newly synthesized relative to total), computed against an unlabelled biomass standard (Methods eq.), not a metabolic flux. Full HSM composition given in Supplementary Data File S2 (not in article XML).

growth_records.medium: Amended HSM (no added organic carbon) in coculture with wild-type Chlamydomonas reinhardtii

up.units: median C_net (fraction of bacterial biomass carbon derived from algal fixed C over 48 h; nanoSIMS 13C APE-derived)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: 13C net carbon assimilation (C_net) into M. japonicum, continuous light, 48 h
mfa.note: Fraction of bacterial cell carbon derived from algal photosynthate; single-cell nanoSIMS, NOT mmol/gDW/h

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: 15N net assimilation (N_net) into M. japonicum in coculture
mfa.note: Much lower than expected N_net (0.63-0.97 if each CFU doubling = biomass doubling), indicating reductive division / starvation; expected values from SI Fig 8B

growth_records.cite: Dupuis, Sunnyjoy et al. Scarcity of fixed carbon transfer in a model microbial phototroph-heterotroph interaction. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae140. [PMC11316394]
growth_records.method: reported (CFU/ml over time; maximum coculture density ~3e7-5e8 CFU/ml, not significantly different from monoculture max density)
growth_records.snippet: more 13C fixed by the alga was taken up by bacterial cells under continuous light ... 0.17 median C_net in continuous light ... measured N_net ... much lower than the expected values at all measured timepoints (0.18-0.34 N_net)
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (M. japonicum) grown with C. reinhardtii as SOLE fixed-carbon source, continuous light, 28 C, aerobic, batch coculture. Experimental single-cell assimilation: 13C C_net median 0.17 and 15N N_net 0.18-0.34 (fractions of newly synthesized biomass), from nanoSIMS SIP; these are NOT mmol/gDW/h fluxes. C_net/N_net computed against unlabelled biomass standards per Methods. Max bacterial density ~3e7-5e8 CFU/ml. Low fixed-carbon transfer = carbon-limited reductive division.

growth_records.medium: Amended HSM (no added organic carbon) in coculture with C. reinhardtii CC-5390, diurnal light

up.compound: algal photosynthetically fixed 13C (from C. reinhardtii CC-5390)
up.units: median C_net (fraction of bacterial biomass C from algal fixed C; nanoSIMS 13C APE-derived)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: 13C net carbon assimilation (C_net) into M. japonicum, diurnal light
mfa.note: Lower than continuous-light C_net (0.17); single-cell nanoSIMS fraction, NOT mmol/gDW/h

growth_records.cite: Dupuis, Sunnyjoy et al. Scarcity of fixed carbon transfer in a model microbial phototroph-heterotroph interaction. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae140. [PMC11316394]
growth_records.method: reported (CFU/ml; C. reinhardtii CC-5390 doubles ~once per day under diurnal light)
growth_records.snippet: 0.17 median C_net in continuous light, 0.09 median C_net [diurnal] ... more 13C fixed by the alga was taken up by bacterial cells under continuous light
growth_records.notes: Same prokaryote/coculture as continuous-light record but under 12L:12D diurnal light with synchronized C. reinhardtii CC-5390. Experimental single-cell 13C C_net median 0.09 (fraction of bacterial C from algal fixed carbon), lower than continuous light 0.17. nanoSIMS SIP fraction, not a flux. No mmol/gDW/h uptake/secretion or numeric growth rate reported.
